license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ba87ihzn
title: Whole Blood Processing (SepMate)
author: Yang Sun

steps_raw.instruction: Purify 20mL or 50mL whole blood using the following protocol:
Centrifuge blood samples at 1200 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes swinging-out rotor and brake off within 06:00:00 of collection.

Carefully transfer 3 mL, plasma from each tube in a 15mL tube avoiding the buffy coat, use remaining blood for PBMC isolation. Note: To minimize PBMC loss during plasma separation, leave the clear layer level at least 1cm above the RBC.

Centrifuge plasma at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes in fixed-angle rotor (if a high-speed centrifuge for 15mL tubes is not available, aliquot plasma in multiple 1.5mL tubes and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, ). Transfer plasma to new tubes avoiding the pellet.
Aliquot 1 mL, plasma in multiple 1.5mL low bind tubes, freeze at -20 °C on the day of collection and move plasma samples to -80 °C after 24:00:00 .
Prepare SepMate tubes with Lymphoprep
Dilute whole blood 1:2 with DPBS (Ca/Mg free). For 105mL total: 35 mL, residual blood and 70 mL, DPBS .

Slowly pipette 35 mL, diluted blood down the side of the tube. Some mixing may occur between the medium and blood, but take care not to mix under the divider. Unlike conventional gradient separation, do not tilt the tube when adding diluted blood.
Spin at 1200 x g, 20 °C, 15 minutes with brakes ON. Prepare three destination 50mL tubes (~30mL per Sepmate tube will be yielded.)
When the spin finishes, pour off the top layer from the SepMate tubes into your prepared destination tubes. Do not invert the tubes for more than 2 seconds.

Prepare 3 SepMate tubes per donor and add 15 mL, Lymphoprep below the insert by pipetting into the center hole. Take care to minimize any air bubbles below the plastic divider. Video from SepMate manufacturers (~2min)
Spin at 450 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brake ON. First wash at higher RCF because gradient medium is mixed with the cells.
Pour off supernatant and combine pellets in three tubes to one, add EasySep buffer to 50mL.

Spin at 350 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brakes ON. If yield is critical, ensure that the supernatant is clear; opaque supernatant indicates incomplete centrifugation of cells. If necessary, perform the spin at 450 x g, °C, .
Pour off supernatant, wash in 50 mL, EasySep buffer for a second wash.
Spin 350 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brakes on.
Pour off supernatant and resuspend in 20 mL, EasySep buffer .
Perform cell count.
Spin at 350 x g, °C, 10 minutes and resuspend in appropriate volume of freezing media A to get 10e7 cells/mL.
Prepare cryovials with 500 µL, freezing media B .
Add 500 µL, cell suspension from previous step to cryovials and gently mix by pipetting.
Place in freezing container for 1 day at -80 °C and move to LN2 tank for long term storage on the following day.
Centrifuge blood samples at 1200 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes swinging-out rotor and brake off within 06:00:00 of collection.

Carefully transfer 3 mL, plasma from each tube in a 15mL tube avoiding the buffy coat, use remaining blood for PBMC isolation. Note: To minimize PBMC loss during plasma separation, leave the clear layer level at least 1cm above the RBC.

Centrifuge plasma at 16000 x g, 4 °C, 10 minutes in fixed-angle rotor (if a high-speed centrifuge for 15mL tubes is not available, aliquot plasma in multiple 1.5mL tubes and centrifuge at 16000 x g, °C, ). Transfer plasma to new tubes avoiding the pellet.
Aliquot 1 mL, plasma in multiple 1.5mL low bind tubes, freeze at -20 °C on the day of collection and move plasma samples to -80 °C after 24:00:00 .
Prepare SepMate tubes with Lymphoprep:

Prepare 2 SepMate tubes per donor and add 15 mL, Lymphoprep below the insert by pipetting into the center hole. Take care to minimize any air bubbles below the plastic divider. Video from SepMate manufacturers (~2min)
Dilute whole blood 1:2 with DPBS (Ca/Mg free). For 42mL total: 14 mL, residual blood and 28 mL, DPBS .

Slowly pipette 21 mL, diluted blood down the side of the tube. Some mixing may occur between the medium and blood, but take care not to mix under the divider. Unlike conventional gradient separation, do not tilt the tube when adding diluted blood.
Spin at 1200 x g, 20 °C, 15 minutes with brakes ON. Prepare one destination 50mL tube (~25mL per Sepmate tube will be yielded.)
When the spin finishes, pour off the top layer from the SepMate tubes into your prepared destination tubes. Do not invert the tubes for more than 2 seconds.
Spin at 450 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brake ON. First wash at higher RCF because gradient medium is mixed with the cells.
Pour off supernatant, add EasySep buffer to 50mL. Can scale washes to 30mL to save reagents.

Spin at 350 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brakes ON. If yield is critical, ensure that the supernatant is clear; opaque supernatant indicates incomplete centrifugation of cells. If necessary, perform the spin at 450 x g, °C, .
Pour off supernatant, wash in 50 mL, EasySep buffer for a second wash.
Spin 350 x g, Room temperature, 10 minutes with brakes on.
Pour off supernatant and resuspend in 20 mL, EasySep buffer .
Perform cell count.
Spin at 350 x g, °C, 10 minutes and resuspend in appropriate volume of freezing media A to get 10e7 cells/mL.
Prepare cryovials with 500 µL, freezing media B .
Add 500 µL, cell suspension from previous step to cryovials and gently mix by pipetting.
Place in freezing container for 1 day at -80 °C and move to LN2 tank for long term storage on the following day.
